market_regime: Risk-on with tech/healthcare leadership; VIX 15.85 complacency; XLK +16.6% 20d; defense tailwinds from Iran/Hormuz; AI infrastructure capex supercycle intact

scanner_blind_spots.reason: Scanner lacks a Starlink-disruption narrative tracker. SpaceX partnership converts the bear thesis into a product line (SuperBroadband) — this is a qualitative thesis insight not detectable from financial screening. At 12.7x Fwd P/E + $11B FCF, it is the clearest large-cap telecom mispricing in the universe.

Scanner doesn't weight Berkshire 13F moves as conviction signals. Berkshire's $2.65B airline re-entry — breaking their 'never again airlines' post-COVID stance — is one of the most powerful institutional quality signals of 2026. This requires human synthesis of the 13F with Berkshire's investment philosophy.

CEO-elect pre-tenure insider buys are not tracked by standard Form 4 monitors (which flag current officers). Michael Weinbach's $9.85M purchase 2 months before officially starting as CEO is a structurally unusual signal type — maximum information advantage combined with maximum personal commitment.

Scanner classifies Constellation Energy as 'utilities' with backward-looking EV/EBITDA metrics. The Three Mile Island restart + 20-year Microsoft PPA creates a structurally protected nuclear baseload moat at premium power purchase prices. Scanner misses the strategic positioning value.

L3Harris is not in the scanner's universe despite being a top-tier defense prime. The pending Axyv missile entity IPO (H2 2026) — where LHX retains 80%+ — is a value unlock not yet in consensus estimates. Multi-expert discovery convergence flagged this independently.

Scanner doesn't model European rearmament as a second-order supply chain tailwind. Germany +24%, Italy +20%, Spain +50% defense budget increases create European OEM production rate increases that flow directly into TransDigm's sole-source proprietary components without new contract awards needed.

Post-Versant spin in January 2026 created a pure broadband + parks company at 6.1x Fwd P/E. Scanner still classifies it as 'cable media' with metrics that include the now-divested content assets. Epic Universe theme park EBITDA +33% is a new growth driver the screen misses.

Scanner lacks a sovereign uranium demand monitor. Trump's $12B Project Vault uranium stockpile initiative + India $2.6B supply deal + Urenco 50% capacity expansion creates multi-source institutional demand. This requires geopolitical reading across energy security + nuclear policy.
